explanation: The LTL property `G(mission -> F explainSuitable)` states that globally, whenever a mission starts, it will eventually be the case that the robot explains why it is suitable for the mission. This captures the essence of the input natural language, which requires the robot to explain its suitability when undertaking a mission. Other properties are not applicable. For example, F explainSuitable is underspecified. G explainSuitable requires explaining suitability at all times, and not just when a mission starts.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot is attempting to start or is currently undertaking a mission (mission), then eventually (F) the robot explains why it believes it's suitable for the mission (explainSuitable).  This captures the prompt by ensuring that, at any point the robot is on a mission, it will eventually provide an explanation of its suitability.

This LTL property states that if the robot is in a mission, then it must eventually explain why it believes it is suitable for the mission. It uses the 'G' (globally) operator to ensure that if the robot is in a mission at any point, then eventually it must explain its suitability. This captures the requirement that the robot explains its suitability. Other possible LTL properties might not capture the time element and dependency between mission start and explanation.
